questions.question: Prove that in a parallelogram, the sum of the squares of the sides equals the sum of the squares of the diagonals.
questions.answer: Using vector or coordinate method, if sides are a and b, and diagonals are p and q, then p² + q² = 2(a² + b²).
answer_outline: Express diagonals in terms of sides

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles and each diagonal bisects opposite angles.
questions.answer: Use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to show right angle intersection and angle bisection.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals and equal opposite sides is a rhombus.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals and given conditions to show all sides are equal.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if and only if the sum of a pair of opposite angles is 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem and its converse to show equivalence.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining midpoints of opposite sides of any quadrilateral forms a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Use vector or coordinate method to show that opposite sides of the new quadrilateral are parallel.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram circumscribed about a circle is a rhombus.
questions.answer: Use tangent lengths from vertices to the circle to show all sides are equal, hence rhombus.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a cyclic quadrilateral is √((s − a)(s − b)(s − c)(s − d)), where s is the semiperimeter.
questions.answer: Use Brahmagupta's formula derived from triangle areas and trigonometry.
answer_outline: Express area in terms of sides

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a kite are perpendicular and one diagonal bisects the other.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show perpendicular intersection and bisection of one diagonal.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if both pairs of opposite sides are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show that equal opposite sides imply diagonals bisect each other, hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that the medians of a quadrilateral divide it into four smaller quadrilaterals of equal area.
questions.answer: Use vector method or coordinate geometry to show equality of areas.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the squares of the diagonals of a parallelogram equals the sum of the squares of all sides plus four times the square of the distance between midpoints of diagonals.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry to express sides and diagonals and apply Pythagoras theorem.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining midpoints of diagonals of a quadrilateral passes through intersection point of the medians.
questions.answer: Use vector method to show that midpoint of diagonals coincides with intersection point of medians.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal and bisect each other.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show equality and bisection.

questions.question: Prove that a rhombus inscribed in a circle is a square.
questions.answer: Use perpendicularity of diagonals and equal sides to show all angles are 90°, hence a square.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a kite is half the product of its diagonals.
questions.answer: Divide kite into four right triangles using diagonals, sum areas = 1/2 × d₁ × d₂.

questions.question: Prove that a trapezium's median line equals half the sum of the bases.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry or midpoints to derive median formula.
answer_outline: Simplify to show half-sum of bases

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rhombus if its diagonals are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show all sides equal, hence rhombus.

questions.question: Prove that a cyclic quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals is a rectangle.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem and properties of diagonals to show all angles are 90°.

questions.question: Prove that in any quadrilateral, the sum of squares of sides equals the sum of squares of diagonals plus four times the square of the line joining midpoints of opposite sides.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry and vector method to derive the relation.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of distances from any interior point to sides of an equilateral triangle is constant.
questions.answer: Use area method to show sum of perpendicular distances equals height of triangle.

questions.question: Prove that in a rectangle, the sum of squares of four sides equals sum of squares of diagonals multiplied by 2.
questions.answer: Use Pythagoras theorem on triangles formed by diagonals and sides.

questions.question: Prove that the centroid of a quadrilateral formed by joining midpoints of sides of any quadrilateral divides it into four equal areas.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry to show intersection point divides quadrilateral into four quadrilaterals of equal area.

questions.question: Prove that a rhomboid has diagonals that bisect each other but are not perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show diagonals bisect each other, angle between them not 90°.

questions.question: Prove that a trapezium is isosceles if and only if its diagonals are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to show equality implies isosceles and vice versa.

questions.question: Prove that in a cyclic quadrilateral, the product of the diagonals equals the sum of the products of opposite sides.
questions.answer: Use Ptolemy's theorem to derive the relation.
